meaning: n.(赶牲口用的)刺棒,激励物,刺激物vt.用刺棒驱赶,驱策,激励,刺激,唆使,煽动
examples: Her presence was just one more goad to Joanna's unravelling nerves.
Theon knew he should not goad her.
They goad Jack to go back inside to be with his family.
"He told his ministers to goad the villagers into speaking out, " says a civil servant.
"If anything, the service is even more popular than our numbers imply, " said Robin Goad.
